primary_pair.axis_differ: I2S substitution (cmplog has it, naive does not) — axis-of-failure: dispatch is opaque to I2S, so the technique adds tax without yield

fuzzer_role.naive: primary pair A — winner. Random walk on data[0] hits TARGET at uniform rate 1/N. Pays only pc-guard tax.
fuzzer_role.cmplog: primary pair B — loser. Same dispatch hit rate as naive, but pays N_NOISE_PER_HANDLER trace_const_cmp4 hooks per non-target execution (16 hooks × (N-1)/N of executions). I2S dictionary fills with noise MAGICs that splice into bytes 4-7, which don't gate dispatch.
fuzzer_role.value_profile: supporting context — should track naive (no I2S, gradient on noise compares doesn't help dispatch).
fuzzer_role.value_profile_cmplog: supporting context — should track cmp or worse (pays both cmp and vp overhead with no compensating signal at dispatch).

expected_direction: naive > cmp at every N_HANDLERS; cmp/naive ratio approximately FLAT across N (throughput tax is per-execution, not per-arm); absolute crash counts scale as 1/N for both fuzzers.

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.naive: monotone-decreasing in N: ~1/N scaling. Highest of the 4 at every N.
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.cmplog: monotone-decreasing in N: ~1/N scaling but multiplied by ~0.2-0.5 (throughput tax).
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile: tracks naive, possibly slightly lower from VP map updates.

acceptance.metric: median crash count per fuzzer per scan value
acceptance.rule: naive median > cmp median at EVERY N_HANDLERS scan value; vp median > vpc median at every N (RBRB shape preserved across the sweep)

design_basis: Function-pointer table indexed by data[0] % N. The dispatch emits NO icmp/switch IR — therefore NO trace_const_cmp4/trace_switch sancov hooks. cmplog's I2S has nothing to substitute for the dispatch tag; only random walk reaches TARGET_TAG. Per-non-target-execution noise compares (16 unhelpful literal-equality CMPs reading bytes 4-7) impose throughput tax on cmp/vpc without giving any useful signal at the dispatch.
notes: Replaces refuted i2s_jump_table_opacity. Original premise (jump-table dispatch invisible to cmplog) was disproven by inspecting LibAFL's __sanitizer_cov_trace_switch hook in libafl_targets/src/sancov_cmp.c:53-87, which iterates all case literals into the cmplog dictionary.
True opacity requires absence of icmp/switch IR — function-pointer dispatch is the canonical example. Other unhookable idioms: C++ vtable, computed goto, JIT-emitted code.

No instance of this mechanism exists in the canonical 5-target DB. Real-world examples are common in C++ codebases (virtual dispatch) and C codebases with method-table patterns (Linux kernel ops structs, OpenSSL EVP, etc.) — not in our currently-instrumented set.
Trap is a direct __builtin_trap() in handler_trap with NO upstream gating — eliminates confound with any other I2S/VP-relevant comparison.
